stories.title: 2,000 years after Pompeii vanished beneath volcanic ash in one of history's deadliest volcanic disasters, the ancient Roman street is blooming with flowers, offering a symbol of nature's renewal
Argentina lost jaguars from its Iberá wetlands nearly 70 years ago, but conservationists have now brought the country's largest wild cat back through an ambitious rewilding project
stories.summary: Jaguars have returned to Argentina's Iberá wetlands after a 70-year absence. Conservationists released a female jaguar and her cubs into Gran Iberá Park. This reintroduction is part of a broader rewilding effort to restore ecosystems. The project aims to re-establish a self-sustaining jaguar population in the region. This initiative helps rebuild the wetland's ecological network and biodiversity.

stories.title: Britain hunted beavers to extinction around 400 years ago, but they are now returning to England's wetlands to rebuild rivers and restore disappearing ecosystems
Why Oklahoma speed-limit signs say “No tolerance,” and what it really means for drivers
stories.summary: Oklahoma's "No Tolerance" signs remind drivers that posted speed limits are absolute. These signs reinforce the state's strict approach to speeding enforcement and traffic safety. Legally, exceeding the speed limit by even one mile per hour is a violation. Law enforcement officers still use discretion in issuing citations for minor infractions. Drivers should treat posted speeds as the actual limit to avoid penalties.

stories.title: NASA’s New Horizons travelled 3 billion miles over 9 years to reach Pluto – but it was moving too fast to stop when it arrived
stories.summary: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft completed its swift flyby of Pluto after a nine-year journey. The mission prioritized data collection over live communication during the brief encounter. Scientists received approximately 50 billion bits of data over fifteen months after the flyby. This slow data return revealed Pluto's complex and geologically active icy world. The mission highlighted the trade-off between reach and the patience required for discovery.

stories.title: Why do we forget why we walked into a room? The fascinating science behind the "Doorway Effect"
stories.summary: Entering a new room can cause temporary memory lapses for your original intention. This doorway effect is a normal brain process for organizing experiences into distinct events. It happens as your brain updates its mental model of the environment. Working memory limitations and attention shifts also contribute to these forgetful moments. Retracing your steps often helps retrieve the forgotten thought by restoring context.

stories.title: Rare fossil reveals T. rex’s attack style
stories.summary: An amazing fossil discovery shows a T. rex tooth stuck in an Edmontosaurus skull, dating back 66 million years. This proves a direct clash between these dinosaurs. The tooth confirms T. rex involvement, meaning the Edmontosaurus was either attacked or scavenged. This is crucial evidence for understanding T. rex's hunting habits.

stories.title: Astronomers have spent decades searching nearby stars for another Earth, and now they may have found one just 25 light-years away
stories.summary: Scientists have found GJ 3378b, a planet near Earth with potential for life. This exoplanet is twice Earth's size and orbits within its star's habitable zone. Researchers used ground-based instruments to update measurements of GJ 3378b's mass. Future telescopes will study its atmosphere to confirm habitability and search for biosignatures. This discovery adds GJ 3378b to the list of promising nearby exoplanets.

stories.title: Meet Carol Ruckdeschel: 84-year-old 'wildest woman in America' who has been living on an island for the 53 years in order to preserve nature for the future
stories.summary: Carol Ruckdeschel has lived on Cumberland Island for 53 years, preserving its natural state. She meticulously documents the island's diverse flora and fauna in her field journals. Her dedication led to legislative changes protecting sea turtles from shrimping trawlers. Ruckdeschel sustains herself by living off the land and off the grid. She continues to advocate for the island's preservation against development proposals.

stories.title: UK seeks law change to deport Pakistani rapist and grooming gang leader Shabir Ahmed

Ethereum outperformed with a 5.3% gain, while the broader crypto market rose 3.2% to $2.22 trillion as investors tracked key macroeconomic and Fed policy cues.

stories.title: Rupee edges higher; options market gauge turns most bearish in over a month
stories.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened modestly on Wednesday against the U.S. dollar. Easing U.S. Federal Reserve rate hike bets supported the rupee's upward movement. Elevated oil prices and Middle East tensions caused trader caution and market anxiety. The Reserve Bank of India's daily reference rate saw significant dollar selling. Bond yields fell after softer U.S. inflation data dampened rate hike expectations.

stories.title: UK to issue first 'digital' bond by early 2027, finance minister says
stories.summary: Britain aims to become the first major economy to issue a digital sovereign bond. This initiative will test distributed-ledger technology for capital markets efficiency. The Bank of England will ensure the digital gilt can be used as collateral. HSBC was selected in February to manage the blockchain platform for this bond. Further issuances are planned after the initial digital gilt sale.

stories.title: China CITIC Bank International plans dollar, offshore yuan perpetual bonds
stories.summary: China CITIC Bank International plans to issue perpetual bonds in US dollars and offshore yuan. Initial price guidance is set around 5.45% for dollar bonds and 2.90% for yuan bonds. These benchmark-sized deals will strengthen the lender's capital base and absorb potential losses. The bank will hold a global investor call later today for these securities. Proceeds from the bonds will fund general corporate purposes after a five-year redemption period.

stories.title: Ather Energy shares rally 6% after Hero MotoCorp approves Rs 1,000 crore additional investment
stories.summary: Ather Energy shares jumped on Wednesday after Hero MotoCorp approved an additional investment of up to Rs 1,000 crore in the EV maker through a preferential allotment, reaffirming its long-term commitment to the company.

stories.title: Wipro Q1 Preview: Under pressure from AI, what can investors expect from earnings?
stories.summary: Wipro's Q1 earnings are expected to show a revenue rise, driven by acquisitions and currency. Core IT services revenue may decline sequentially, impacting overall profitability. Analysts anticipate margin pressure from wage increases and deal ramp-ups. Investors will focus on September quarter guidance and deal conversion rates. Management commentary on demand and AI strategy will be closely watched.

stories.title: Ashish Kacholia cuts stake in two chemical stocks after 114% rally. What's driving the move?
stories.summary: Ace investor Ashish Kacholia has reduced holdings in two chemical stocks. Yasho Industries and Fineotex Chemicals saw stake reductions by the investor. This move occurred after substantial stock price rallies earlier this year. Supply chain issues and Chinese competition are impacting the chemical sector. Kacholia also reduced his stake in NBFC SG Finserve.

stories.title: IDBI Bank shares jump 3% as Fairfax set to make biggest foreign investment in Indian lender, buy stake worth Rs 53,000 cr
stories.summary: Fairfax Holdings has been finalized for the IDBI Bank stake sale. This significant deal is expected to be worth approximately Rs 53,000 crore. The government plans to sell its stake, and LIC will also divest shares. Fairfax's offer of Rs 81 per share is higher than last year's bid. This transaction marks the largest foreign investment in an Indian bank.

stories.title: Tata Elxsi among 5 F&O stocks with a sharp rise in futures open interest
stories.summary: Five NSE F&O stocks witnessed a sharp rise in futures open interest on July 14, signalling increased market participation and fresh position-building. Tata Elxsi led the list with a 16.35% jump in OI, followed by HCL Technologies, Radico Khaitan, IREDA and L&T Finance.

stories.title: Global Market: China Q2 growth slows to 4.3% yoy; Asia, metals stocks likely to remain volatile
stories.summary: China's economy grew 4.3% year-on-year in the second quarter, missing market expectations and marking its slowest pace since late 2022. The weaker-than-expected growth, driven by soft domestic demand despite resilient exports and industrial production, could keep Asian equities and metal stocks volatile as investors assess the outlook for China's economy and potential policy stimulus.

stories.title: US Stock Market: Citigroup shares slide after upbeat results fail to ease worries over higher costs
stories.summary: Citigroup reported strong second-quarter earnings, driven by trading and investment banking revenues. The bank's net income jumped 45%, surpassing analyst expectations significantly. However, shares declined as investors focused on potential higher expenses ahead. Citigroup maintained its full-year return target, signaling possible weaker second-half profitability. The bank's results align with other major U.S. financial institutions reporting robust profits.

stories.title: Eternal, ICICI Bank, DMart among 9 largecap stocks with up to 34% upside. See full list
stories.summary: Axis Securities forecasts a Nifty target of 27,220 by December 2026. The brokerage highlights nine largecap stocks with substantial upside potential. These include Avenue Supermarts, offering a 34.5% potential gain. Bharti Airtel and Varun Beverages also show strong projected growth. ICICI Bank and Kotak Mahindra Bank are among other recommended stocks.

stories.title: Just 2% return! Will SBI Funds Management break the curse of India's poor billion-dollar IPO record?
stories.summary: India's large IPOs have shown mixed investor returns, with many billion-dollar offerings underperforming. SBI Funds Management's IPO will now test this trend with its strong financials. Analysis shows eight of thirteen large IPOs are trading above their issue prices. However, several major offerings like New India Assurance have seen significant losses. The success of SBI Funds Management's IPO hinges on future earnings growth supporting its valuation.

stories.title: Bonus issue alert! Last day to buy this microcap pharma stock that rallied 200% in one year
stories.summary: Fredun Pharmaceuticals has fixed July 16 as the record date for its 2:1 bonus issue, making today the last day to buy the stock to qualify. The microcap pharma stock has surged nearly 200% over the past year and 84% in 2026, reflecting strong investor interest and robust business momentum.

Quote of the day by Stoic philosopher Epictetus: "Any person capable of angering you becomes your master; he can anger you only when…" - why losing control of your emotions can hand power to someone else
stories.summary: Stoic philosopher Epictetus taught that anger is a choice, not an automatic reaction. He believed individuals control their responses to external provocations. This ancient wisdom remains relevant in today's digital age. Modern life offers many opportunities for emotional entanglement with others. Recognizing this power dynamic helps maintain personal control over one's day.

Nepal court jails ex-deputy PM, home minister in fake refugee scam; 14 others convicted
stories.summary: Two former Nepal ministers received prison sentences for their involvement in a fake refugee document scam. Top Bahadur Rayamajhi was sentenced to four years, and Bal Krishna Khand received two years. Fourteen others were also convicted and sentenced for their roles in the alleged fraud. The investigation began in 2023 after authorities uncovered claims of Nepali citizens posing as refugees.

stories.title: NSE partners with Augmont to deepen electronic gold market, reduce reliance on imports
stories.summary: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) has partnered with Augmont Enterprises to boost the adoption of Electronic Gold Receipts (EGRs), aiming to build a deeper and more liquid spot gold market in India. The collaboration seeks to formalise the country's vast household gold holdings by enabling investors to trade, lend and redeem physical gold through a regulated exchange framework, while improving liquidity, price discovery and market efficiency.

stories.title: Dolly Khanna portfolio rejig: Ace investor likely exits 2 stocks, pares holding in Prakash Industries in Q1
stories.summary: Ace investor Dolly Khanna likely exited Rain Industries and Sharda Cropchem during the June quarter after strong gains of up to 42% this year, while trimming her stake in Prakash Industries, according to the latest shareholding data.

stories.title: Tech Mahindra Q1 Preview: Will the IT company surprise investors with its earnings?
stories.summary: Tech Mahindra is expected to post modest June-quarter revenue growth, led by continued strength in its telecom business, while margins are likely to improve on cost optimisation. Investors will closely track management commentary on AI strategy, telecom demand, large deal execution, BFSI recovery and progress towards its FY27 margin target.

stories.title: Chipmaker CXMT eyes $9.8 bn in blockbuster China IPO
stories.summary: China's leading memory chipmaker ChangXin Memory Technologies is seeking billions in an IPO. This move aims to bolster the nation's artificial intelligence hardware capabilities. The company intends to rival global giants like Samsung and Micron. This significant offering marks China's largest mainland tech share sale ever. It will provide a strong foundation for China's AI development goals.

stories.title: Global Market: European shares slip as AI chip rally fails to offset Middle East concerns
stories.summary: European shares dipped slightly as Middle East tensions grew. Technology stocks advanced, boosted by ASML's positive financial forecasts. Luxury shares also performed well, driven by strong demand for jewellery. However, broader market sentiment remained cautious due to geopolitical risks. Investors are now closely watching corporate earnings for economic insights.

stories.title: Patanjali Foods shares crash 20%, stock nearly halves in value in one year. What's ahead?
stories.summary: Patanjali Foods shares plunged 20% to a fresh 52-week low, extending their decline to nearly 50% from last year's peak. While the company said there were no material developments behind the sharp fall, technical analysts warned of further downside, with the next key support seen in the Rs 325–330 zone.

stories.title: Warren Buffett and Bill Gates no longer friends? Why legendary investor skipped donations to Gates foundation
stories.summary: Warren Buffett is donating nearly six billion dollars of Berkshire Hathaway stock this year. His annual mid-year donation went to four family foundations, not Gates Foundation. Speculation arose after revelations about Bill Gates' interactions with Jeffrey Epstein. Buffett previously committed to donating shares throughout his life by 2034. He stepped down as a Gates Foundation trustee in 2021.

stories.title: Global Market: ECB monitoring Middle East conflict for inflation risks
stories.summary: The European Central Bank sees no immediate inflation fallout from the Iran conflict. Policymakers are closely watching for indirect price pressures and their potential impact. The eurozone economy has shown resilience despite ongoing geopolitical tensions and uncertainty. Medium and long-term inflation expectations remain well anchored, reflecting confidence. The bank stands ready to adjust policy if inflation risks increase significantly.

stories.title: HDFC Bank, SBI, other stocks jump up to 2%; Nifty Bank gains 560 points. What lies ahead?
stories.summary: Banking stocks rallied up to 2% on Wednesday, lifting the Nifty Bank index as investors positioned themselves ahead of a crucial round of Q1 earnings from major private lenders this weekend. Brokerages remain constructive on the sector, citing improving fundamentals, resilient loan growth and stable asset quality, while cautioning that pressure on net interest margins could weigh on near-term profitability.

stories.title: 10-year bond yield may trade in 6.60-6.90% range over the next month: PGIM India MF's Puneet Pal
stories.summary: PGIM India's Puneet Pal expects the 10-year bond yield to trade between 6.60-6.90% over the next month, citing global rate hikes and a deficient monsoon, even as he favours the short end of the curve due to improving banking liquidity and possible FCNR-led inflows.

stories.title: Japan PM Takaichi says draft economic blueprint not cause of bond market rout
stories.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi stated no connection exists between the economic blueprint and market volatility. She noted interest rates and foreign exchange depend on various global economic factors. Concerns about political influence on monetary policy have recently surfaced. The draft economic blueprint emphasized appropriate monetary policy guidance for economic strength. This guidance aims to foster a more robust and stable Japanese economy.

stories.title: Q1 results today: HDFC Life Insurance, HDB Financial, Jana SFB, ICICI Prudential among 43 companies to report earnings
stories.summary: The June quarter earnings season gathers pace with 43 companies, including HDFC Life Insurance, HDB Financial Services, Jana Small Finance Bank and ICICI Prudential Life Insurance, set to report results today. Around 143 companies will announce earnings this week, with investors tracking corporate performance, demand trends and sectoral outlooks.

stories.title: Euro zone bond yields tick higher along with oil prices after big swings
stories.summary: Euro zone bond yields edged higher on Wednesday as oil prices climbed. Germany's 2-year bond yield rose, sensitive to central bank rate expectations. Oil prices increased as the U.S. and Iran continued trading strikes. Money markets are pricing in further ECB tightening this year. U.S. inflation data showed a slowdown in June.

stories.title: Groww shares gain 8% on strong Q1 earnings, net profit jumps 94% to Rs 735 crore
stories.summary: Billionbrains Garage Ventures shares surged after reporting strong Q1 FY27 earnings. The company posted a consolidated net profit of Rs 735 crore, up 94.44% year-on-year. Revenue from operations also rose significantly, driven by traction in newer products. Groww retained its position as India's largest direct mutual fund distribution platform. The company added net clients and expanded market share across key segments.

stories.title: Global Market: BoE governor calls for balanced banking rules, rejects deregulation push
stories.summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey pushed back against broad calls for deregulation, arguing that effective financial rules are essential for maintaining stability and supporting long-term economic growth. He also renewed his call for greater international cooperation on regulating advanced AI models, warning that the technology's cross-border risks require coordinated oversight.

stories.title: Indian real estate attracts $3.2 billion PE investments in H1 2026, up 33% YoY: Savills India
stories.summary: Private equity investments in India's real estate sector rose 33% year-on-year to $3.2 billion in the first half of 2026, driven by strong interest in data centres, office assets and alternative real estate segments, according to Savills India. Domestic investors accounted for a majority of the inflows, while foreign capital remained focused on data centres and hospitality.

stories.title: Global Market: Japan bond market sees steeper curve amid yield divergence
stories.summary: Japanese government bond yields saw a steepening curve on Wednesday. Shorter-dated yields declined, mirroring U.S. Treasury movements after inflation data. Longer-dated yields, however, climbed due to fiscal and inflation concerns. This occurred after a strong auction of 20-year bonds influenced market sentiment. Investors are watching pension fund allocation changes for future market direction.

stories.title: Explained: Why is stock market rising today even as Iran-US war escalates, oil prices cross $85
stories.summary: Indian equities rallied on Wednesday despite escalating Iran-US tensions and Brent crude crossing $85 a barrel, as investors bet the conflict will remain contained and global risk appetite improves on softer US inflation. Financial stocks led the gains, though analysts cautioned that geopolitical risks, inflation and oil prices remain key headwinds.

stories.title: Tata Capital gives dollar bond guidance, final price by this week, bankers say
stories.summary: Tata Capital is planning a new U.S. dollar bond issuance. The company seeks to raise between $400 million and $600 million. This debt offering will mature in three-and-a-half years. Proceeds will fund onward lending and other eligible activities. This marks Tata Capital's second dollar debt sale.

stories.title: Vietnam raises $116 million in government bond auction
stories.summary: Vietnam's State Treasury successfully raised three trillion dong in its recent bond auction. This amount represents an increase from the previous week's successful bond sales. The treasury sold a portion of its five-year and ten-year bonds on offer. Additionally, some fifteen-year and thirty-year bonds were sold while three-year bonds saw no sales. The government continues its efforts to meet its annual bond sale target.

stories.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak tops 2,000 cases, kills 754 in fastest-growing surge
stories.summary: In the Democratic Republic of Congo, the Ebola situation has worsened, now exceeding two thousand confirmed infections and recording 754 lives lost. This outbreak has become the most rapid on record, overwhelming health authorities. Reports of suspected cases in additional provinces suggest wider transmission, though new treatment trials targeting the Bundibugyo strain provide potential avenues for relief amid severe challenges in containment.

stories.title: 6 most snake-filled water bodies in South Carolina: From the Savannah River to the ACE Basin
stories.summary: South Carolina's abundant waters attract many snake species, both harmless and dangerous. The Savannah River and Great Pee Dee River are home to several notable snake populations. Congaree River and Lake Murray host a variety of snakes, including venomous ones. Lake Hartwell and the ACE Basin also feature numerous water-dwelling snake species. These warm environments and plentiful prey make South Carolina ideal for snakes.

Swiss cows wear giant bells for a simple reason rooted in mountain life
stories.summary: In the picturesque Alps, cowbells play a crucial role for farmers overseeing their herds. These melodic bells make it easier to track cattle across expansive mountain landscapes, especially amid fluctuating weather conditions. Local traditions like Alpabzug honor this custom, showcasing beautifully adorned bells as part of cattle returning home. Interestingly, studies suggest cows become accustomed to these sounds, influencing their behavior.

stories.title: How tiny pollen grains helped solve a brutal murder: The fascinating science behind nature's invisible crime scene evidence
stories.summary: Tiny pollen grains offer vital clues for investigators solving complex criminal cases. Forensic palynologists analyze these microscopic particles to link suspects to crime scenes. A notable murder case in New Zealand was solved using unusual mutated pollen evidence. Pollen's resistance to decomposition makes it a persistent and valuable forensic tool. This silent evidence helps identify and locate regions, aiding in criminal investigations.

Examining fear's signal, rather than hiding it, is key to its effective management.

How elephants turn footsteps into messages
stories.summary: Through the ground, elephants send and receive vibrations, a unique way of communicating that surpasses traditional airborne sounds. Their anatomy, featuring sizable middle ear components and eardrums, allows for the efficient transfer of low-frequency signals to the cochlea. Intriguingly, they can also close their ear canals at will, amplifying their ability to perceive these low frequencies. This remarkable skill unveils the depths of their sophisticated communication systems.

stories.title: A 1971 prediction by Stephen Hawking just came true with a cosmic collision 1.3 billion light-years away: Now scientists are trying to understand how
stories.summary: A cosmic collision of two black holes confirmed Stephen Hawking's fifty-year-old prediction. The observed increase in the merged black hole's area precisely matched theoretical calculations. This event provided the most accurate observational evidence for Hawking's black hole area law. Scientists collaborated across LIGO, Virgo, and KAGRA to analyze the gravitational wave signal. The precise data reinforces fundamental principles of modern physics and cosmology.

stories.title: Quote of the day by Augustus: 'We write our names in the sand and then the waves roll in and...'
stories.summary: A thought-provoking quote from Neil Gaiman's comic series suggests that names written in sand reflect life's transient beauty, although it is incorrectly credited to Emperor Augustus Caesar. This notion echoes ancient philosophical themes of mortality and the futility of permanence. In contrast to the perceived eternal nature of digital existence, recognizing impermanence can free us from the burden of seeking to leave an indelible mark.

stories.title: Quote of the day by Cyrus the Great: 'All men have their frailties; and whoever looks for a friend...'
stories.summary: Cyrus the Great, an ancient king, cautioned against the pursuit of flawless friendships. He recognized that accepting human imperfections contributed to the foundation of a strong empire. In today's world, technology magnifies our quest for perfect relationships, often resulting in loneliness. This unrealistic pursuit stifles meaningful connections. By embracing imperfections, we can nurture deeper and more resilient bonds with one another.

stories.title: This billionaire lived six months a year on his 233-foot superyacht to avoid paying land taxes
stories.summary: Innovative Microsoft engineer Charles Simonyi cleverly leveraged a bespoke superyacht to sidestep local taxation and stringent construction regulations. This mobile abode enabled him to traverse the globe while circumventing substantial property tax liabilities. His forward-thinking strategy predates ongoing discussions surrounding wealth taxation in high-levy regions. A trailblazer in digital technology and space tourism, he eventually acquired an even grander superyacht to enhance his aquatic adventures.
